reviews.review_description: Over the past year or so I have tried most of the free VR apps available within the GearVR world.  This was the most bizarre, amazing and exciting execution of what the future holds for VR.  Music, colors, interactive, artistic... great job.
Definetely brainwash.. sketchy demonic. Do not watch this. Who ever mde this is straight sketchy
I really enjoyed this and the sense of interaction. Super wierd but equally entertaining and humorous. Great job..more please

As other reviewer stated it makes you nauseous after a couple of minutes viewing, it's a great way to promote a track and I have watched these sort of music videos in VR before but this one was just too weird and I suffered a lot of motion sickness.
honestly one of the best apps on here!
Started out pretty interesting but the « video glitching effect » was very uncomfortable to me.  The song is pretty repetitive.  Not a very good experience, glad I didn’t have to pay for it.
I had to shut it off as it made me really dizzy. Too much nonsensical stuff going on, really confusing and weird.  The baby dude freaked me out.
I found this trippy and cool!

Definitely one of the most impressive VR experiences I've seen yet. If you want freaky-deaky intensely strange eye-candy, look no further.
While some of the lurid themes in this aren't exactly my cup of tea, this still delivers a phantasmagorical journey in spades. Will be on the lookout for more stuff from the developer for sure.
Very odd and trippy - not really sure what to say

That was seriously weird and trippy!
I really recommend this experience.

reviews.review_title: IF only it were any good
reviews.review_description: For the record, I’m not at all opposed to any art that could be categorized as freaky, bizarre, or even downright scary, especially when it comes to music videos. Being that these are short promos that enhance a musical experience visually, such adjectives often make for amazingly disquieting experiences. One of the most unforgettable videos of my younger days was Aphex Twin’s “Come To Daddy.” This was the stuff nightmares were truly made of, set to a track that could be described as anything but pleasant. The creators of IF would seem to have taken a page from that playbook, but unfortunately they have failed. It’s not enough to create unpleasant imagery; it has to have an iconic connection to our collective unconscious or has to capture the zeitgeist of its time. Otherwise, it’s just simply unpleasant. To echo what earlier reviewers have stated, this video suffers from a horrible glitching effect that is probably meant to induce paranoia, but instead just induces nausea. The images of abstract nudity and psychological mayhem carried no charge for me, and left me just waiting for it all to end. As a whole, I felt no intellectual or emotional connection, nor did I get any sense of staying power or statement on our current climate. I know not all entertainment has to be artistic and profound, but since entertainment does often center around beauty and appeal, usually something so pervasively ugly does carry a message. Any underlying provocations were lost on me, and even if they were there, I don’t really care to go back and try to figure it out.
A  stunning visceral experience that will blow your mind. Excellent work with the soundtrack paired with stunning visuals !!
